title: No Diet Day: Celebrate Your Body!
story: Every May 6, people around the world celebrate International No Diet Day by ditching the scale and embracing body positivity! This special day encourages everyone to eat what makes them happy without guilt or worry about dieting. People share uplifting messages on social media, enjoy their favorite foods with friends, and focus on being healthy rather than skinny. Many schools and communities host events that teach kids about loving their bodies just as they are. It's a day to remember that all bodies are good bodies, and happiness matters more than fitting into certain clothes or reaching a magic number on the scale.
activity_question: What is the main message of International No Diet Day?
activity_choices: All bodies are good bodies and happiness matters most
People should weigh themselves every day
Only skinny people can be healthy
activity_answer: All bodies are good bodies and happiness matters most
follow_up_question: What's one way you could show kindness to yourself and your body today?

title: Flipping Out for Fancy French Pancakes
story: Chefs around the world show off their fanciest pancake skills on National Crepe Suzette Day every May 6! These aren't your regular breakfast pancakes - crepe suzette is a thin French pancake covered in buttery orange sauce that gets set on fire right at your table. Restaurants love putting on this dramatic show, and cooking classes teach people how to flip the delicate crepes without tearing them. Some brave home cooks try making the flaming dessert themselves, though most skip the fire part for safety!
activity_question: What makes crepe suzette different from regular pancakes?
activity_choices: It's covered in orange sauce and set on fire
It's eaten with a fork and spoon only
activity_answer: It's covered in orange sauce and set on fire
follow_up_question: What other foods do you think would be exciting to prepare with flames?

title: Make a Wish and Break It!
story: Every May 6, people celebrate Wishbone Day by cracking turkey wishbones and making wishes! Families save the V-shaped bone from their turkey dinner, let it dry out, then two people grab each end and pull until it snaps. Whoever gets the bigger piece wins and their wish might come true! Some people collect wishbones all year just for this special day. Others share photos of their wishbone battles on social media, showing off their winning pieces and keeping their wishes secret for good luck.
activity_question: What happens to the person who gets the bigger piece of the wishbone?
bonus_fact: The wishbone tradition started with ancient Romans over 2,000 years ago!
